meta.description: A specific, identifiable legislative initiative — a proposed law, reform, decree, regulation, legislative agreement, or ratification — filed before a legislative body. Each initiative is a named, attribute-bearing entity distinguishable by its title, proponents, jurisdiction, and filing date. An article refers to a legislative_initiative when it reports on or discusses a specific, identifiable proposal (e.g. a particular bill with a title or author, a specific reform being debated, a named decree), not when it covers general legislative activity, political commentary, or the broader theme of lawmaking.

example.name: Reforma al Poder Judicial
aliases: Iniciativa de reforma al Poder Judicial de la Federacion
example.description: Iniciativa de reforma constitucional presentada por el Ejecutivo Federal para modificar la estructura del Poder Judicial, incluyendo la eleccion por voto popular de ministros, magistrados y jueces.
example.context: La iniciativa forma parte de un paquete de reformas enviadas al Congreso al inicio del nuevo periodo legislativo. Ha generado amplio debate entre juristas y partidos de oposicion.

date_planned_vote.mention: la votacion esta prevista para la primera semana de septiembre

affected_laws: Constitucion Politica de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos
example.vote_result: aprobada en lo general con 357 votos a favor

entity_type.description: Type of legislative initiative. Choose the single most specific category that matches.

name.description: Short name or title of the initiative, e.g. 'Reforma al Poder Judicial', 'Ley Federal de Proteccion al Consumidor', 'Decreto de extincion de fideicomisos'.

aliases.description: Alternative names or short forms used to refer to the initiative (e.g. 'Reforma judicial', 'Ley de ingresos 2025').

description.description: Brief description of what the initiative proposes or modifies.

tags.description: Keywords describing the initiative and the areas of law it touches.

context.description: Contextual information: political context, debate, related initiatives, historical background.

relevance.description: Relevance of the initiative within the article: 1 = main topic, 2 = secondary, 3 = implicit.

current_stage.description: Specific procedural stage of the initiative, if reported.

jurisdiction.description: Geographic jurisdiction the initiative applies to (country, state, municipality). Fill the hierarchical fields that match the scope (e.g. federal initiative → country only; state reform → country + state).

legislative_body.description: Legislative body where the initiative was filed (e.g. 'Congreso de la Union', 'Camara de Diputados', 'Senado de la Republica', 'Congreso del Estado de Guanajuato', 'Cabildo de Leon').

date_introduced.description: Date the initiative was filed or introduced. If the article only mentions an approximate period, estimate precision_days accordingly.

date_planned_vote.description: Date the initiative is scheduled or expected to be voted on, if mentioned (e.g. 'la votacion esta prevista para el proximo martes'). Leave null if the article does not mention a planned vote date. If only an approximate period is mentioned, estimate precision_days accordingly.

authors.description: Proponents or authors of the initiative (legislators, executive, political parties, civic groups). Include name, role, and organization when available.

parties_involved.description: Political parties that proposed, supported, or opposed the initiative (e.g. 'Morena', 'PAN', 'PRI', 'MC', 'PT', 'PVEM').

legislators_supporting.description: Individual legislators explicitly reported as supporting or voting in favor of the initiative, with name, role (e.g. 'Senador', 'Diputada'), and organization (political party or chamber).

legislators_against.description: Individual legislators explicitly reported as opposing or voting against the initiative, with name, role (e.g. 'Senador', 'Diputada'), and organization (political party or chamber).

topic_areas.description: Areas of law or policy the initiative addresses (e.g. 'fiscal', 'labor', 'environmental', 'electoral', 'criminal_code', 'education').

affected_laws.description: Existing laws, codes, or constitutional articles that the initiative modifies, repeals, or supplements (e.g. 'Constitucion Politica de los Estados Unidos Mexicanos', 'Codigo Fiscal de la Federacion, articulo 28').

identifiers.description: Official identifiers for the initiative (expediente number, folio, gazette reference, etc.).

vote_result.description: Vote outcome as reported, if any (e.g. '357 votos a favor, 98 en contra', 'aprobada por mayoria calificada').

text_url.description: URL to the official text of the initiative, if mentioned.

related_subjects.description: Other subjects or domains touched by the initiative (e.g. 'rule_of_law', 'human_rights', 'economics', 'public_health', 'migration').
